findings.description: The NC Energy, Mineral & Land Resources logo appears in the HTML but with slightly different rendering/positioning compared to the PDF header.
findings.evidence: PDF shows logo in upper left with text 'Energy, Mineral & Land Resources ENVIRONMENTAL QUALITY' beneath it. HTML shows similar logo but positioning differs slightly.

findings.description: The outline items (I-IV) in the PDF use Roman numerals with tab-aligned text, while the HTML renders them slightly differently in terms of indentation.
findings.evidence: PDF shows Roman numerals I-IV with consistent tab stops. HTML shows similar structure but with different indentation alignment.

findings.description: Page 2 in the HTML appears to be mostly blank with just the header/footer repeated and then content continues, matching the PDF's blank space.
findings.evidence: PDF page 2 is mostly blank space. HTML page 2 similarly shows mostly blank space with header elements.

findings.description: The bullet list items in the CAMA process section use different bullet styles between PDF and HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF uses plain bullet dots (•), HTML uses standard list bullets. Content is the same.

findings.description: Bold topic labels (Environmental, Environmental Justice, etc.) in the oral comments section are preserved in HTML but the bullet style differs slightly.
findings.evidence: PDF uses filled circle bullets (•), HTML uses standard list item bullets. Bold labels are maintained in both.

findings.description: Section numbering in Attachments list uses Roman numerals with different spacing in HTML vs PDF.
findings.evidence: PDF shows 'I.' through 'VI.' with aligned indentation. HTML shows similar numbering but with slightly different alignment.

findings.description: The document title 'MEMORANDUM' is set as h1, which is acceptable, but the main section headings (I, II, III, IV) are set as h2, which is also fine. However, there is a nested <main> element inside another <main>, which is a structural HTML issue but not a heading hierarchy problem per se.
findings.evidence: HTML has <main> nested inside <main>. This is an HTML structure issue, not a heading issue.

findings.description: Several images have empty alt text, which is generic rather than descriptive. The first image has reasonable alt text ('NC Energy, Mineral and Land Resources logo'), but subsequent images have empty alt attributes.
findings.evidence: HTML: alt="" on images 2 through 5. These appear to be page-level images or decorative elements from the PDF.

findings.description: The page footer text is repeated multiple times in the HTML, once per original PDF page. This is acceptable as it mirrors the source PDF's per-page footer, but it creates redundancy in the HTML output.
findings.evidence: The footer 'State of North Carolina | Environmental Quality | Energy, Mineral and Land Resources...' appears 5 times in the HTML.
